content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Reviewed the stdout-only exhaustion classification fix across process_backend.rs and tmux.rs. The implementation is correct and well-scoped: (1) New `stdout_for_exit_failure_classification` helper properly prefers structured stdout error and falls back to a bounded raw-stdout tail (4096 bytes via STDERR_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT), consistent with how stderr is already handled. (2) All three call sites (two in process_backend, one in tmux) are consistently updated. (3) Two new exhaustion patterns ('insufficient credits'/'insufficient credit') are reasonable additions. (4) Test coverage is comprehensive — unit tests for the helper function, an inline test for end-to-end classification, and integration tests for both ProcessBackendAdapter and TmuxAdapter exercising the full path with a real stubbed process. (5) Docstrings for `classify_exit_failure_with_output` are correctly updated to reflect the new behavior. (6) The false-positive risk from model conversation content is mitigated by bounding the scan to the last 4096 bytes, matching the existing stderr strategy. Pre-existing clippy warnings exist in other files but none were introduced by this change. No bugs, safety issues, or correctness gaps found.

## Amendments

None.
